definition: The portable project-agnostic Claude Code agent setup contained in this repo; installs as <project>/.claude/.
The per-cwd memory daemon (mcp__kern__*); the machine's durable memory — never file-memory or context-mode.
The portable machine — identical in every repo; skills/agents/hooks/rules/settings.
The project layer — per-repo specialization regenerated by /oil-me; never copied between repos.
The always-applies rules in agents/default.md (root cause
Per-repo binding rules in /.machine/agent.md; here sourced from coding-standards.md.
The single lifecycle skill that installs/updates the machine and re-indexes /.machine/ by scanning the current repo; replaces /bootstrap.
Slash-command files must be routing wrappers under 20 LOC that invoke a skill via the Skill tool.
Agents resolve by name: frontmatter not path; names must be unique and skills: refs must resolve.
Live worklist in /.machine/ (tracked in git) for the machine-unification /improve loop; holds outstanding work only and shrinks toward empty.
A numbered unit of the unification sweep; referenced in improve.json and commit messages (e.g. "Slice 6").
Only one session may drive the /improve loop; improve.json has no lock and concurrent writers lose updates (incident e193515d).
The machine's code-quality bar defined in the foundation-quality skill; enforced by manager-quality and /gate.
The data-driven review panel defined by files in /.machine/personas/; run in parallel via /personas after non-trivial work.
The ordered set of per-task entry-files under /.machine/sessions/ that the orchestrator schedules and fires; the set of files IS the board (no monolithic file).

The named configurable countdown between adding a task and its auto-fire; read from the MACHINE_SETTLE_DELAY_MINUTES parameter in settings.json (the single source for its value); a scheduling parameter not an effort estimate.
The absolute timestamp (added_at + settle_delay) at which a settling task becomes eligible to auto-fire.
The state of a scheduled task whose fire_at has not yet passed; its timer is counting down.
A user-applied indefinite pause of a task's timer; a frozen task never fires and is excluded from wakeup scheduling until unfrozen.
The per-task flag marking that a task writes files and must run in worktree isolation so concurrent writers cannot collide on the working tree.
The guard that gates auto-fire on driver ownership rather than on the settle timer alone; only entry-files the driver created and tracks this session may auto-fire
column4: so an injected or stale entry cannot.

definition: The status of an entry-file under /.machine/sessions/ that is not in the driver's in-session tracked set (foreign or stale); quarantined — never auto-fired and surfaced in the footer for human review.
The user command that resolves an untrusted entry by moving it into the driver's tracked set and re-scheduling it (status scheduled
column4: fire_at recomputed) so it follows the normal lifecycle under the driver's ownership.

definition: The optional staging status of a task drafted mid-clarification
column4: not yet on the timed board and carrying no countdown.

definition: The transient status of a task whose redo SendMessage has been sent and whose agent response is pending; becomes running once the agent re-engages.
The explicit frontmatter priority field that deterministically breaks precedence ties among equally-eligible tasks; lower order fires first and never overrides dependencies or freeze.
